steps.title: Read where the graph crosses the $x$-axis
steps.content: The values of $x$ for which $f(x) = 0$ are exactly the $x$-intercepts of the graph — points where the curve crosses the $x$-axis.

steps.title: Count the $x$-intercepts
steps.content:
The graph approaches a horizontal asymptote and never touches the $x$-axis — there are no $x$-intercepts. So $f(x) = 0$ has zero solutions.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
